question_text: A factory decides to replace old machinery (60% efficient) with new machinery (88% efficient). If both machines perform the same amount of useful work (4400 kJ per day), how much more fuel energy must the old machine consume compared to the new machine?
correct_answer: Old machine requires 7333 kJ, new machine requires 5000 kJ. Difference: 2333 kJ more fuel needed for old machine.
explanation: For old machine: useful work = 60% × input, so 4400 = 0.60 × input, therefore input = 4400 ÷ 0.60 = 7333 kJ. For new machine: 4400 = 0.88 × input, so input = 4400 ÷ 0.88 = 5000 kJ. Difference = 7333 - 5000 = 2333 kJ. This shows why upgrading to efficient machinery saves fuel and money.
